mission.title: Install and Configure MySQL Operator for Kubernetes (Oracle)
mission.description: The MySQL Operator for Kubernetes automates the management of MySQL InnoDB Cluster setups within a Kubernetes environment. It simplifies tasks such as deployment, scaling, and backup, making it ideal for users who need a robust database solution in a containerized environment. Key trade-offs include the complexity of operator management versus the ease of manual database administration.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MySQL Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the operator easily.

```bash

helm repo add mysql-operator https://mysql.github.io/mysql-operator/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official MySQL Operator Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MySQL Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the MySQL Operator in a new namespace called 'mysql-operator'.

```bash

helm install mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator --create-namespace

```

> This deploys the MySQL Operator using default configurations.

steps.title: Verify the Operator is Running
steps.description:
After installation, it's important to verify that the MySQL Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> Check the status of the MySQL Operator deployment.

steps.title: Create a Secret for MySQL Credentials
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes secret that stores the MySQL root user credentials required for the InnoDB Cluster.

```bash

kubectl create secret generic mypwds --from-literal=rootUser=root --from-literal=rootHost=% --from-literal=rootPassword: <REDACTED>'

```

> This secret will be referenced in the InnoDB Cluster configuration.

steps.title: Define and Deploy MySQL InnoDB Cluster
steps.description:
Define the InnoDB Cluster resource that uses the previously created secret for authentication.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
EOF

```

> This command creates the InnoDB Cluster using a YAML definition.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
EOF

steps.title: Verify the InnoDB Cluster is Ready
steps.description:
Check the status of the InnoDB Cluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster

```

> This command retrieves the status of the InnoDB Cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MySQL Operator
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the MySQL Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator

kubectl delete namespace mysql-operator

```

> This command removes the MySQL Operator deployment.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MySQL Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the MySQL Operator to the latest version.

```bash

helm upgrade mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> This command upgrades the MySQL Operator to the latest version.
> Verify the deployment status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to MySQL server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MySQL server may not be running or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the MySQL Operator logs and verify the secret credentials.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'InnoDB Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The InnoDB Cluster resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Ensure the InnoDB Cluster YAML was applied correctly and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to create resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may not have the necessary RBAC permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the ClusterRole and ensure it has the correct permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release may have been deleted or not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Helm chart or check the release name.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for MySQL 8.4, improved backup functionalities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from 8.3.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MySQL 9.0, enhanced performance metrics.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure to update your InnoDB Cluster configurations to align with new features.

resolution.summary: You should see the InnoDB Cluster status as 'Ready' when running `kubectl get innodbclusters.mysql.oracle.com mycluster`.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
